description: Iraq invaded Kuwait triggering US-led coalition response. Oil spiked 130% in months.
September 11 attacks caused brief oil spike followed by demand concerns.
US-led invasion of Iraq. Oil rose steadily from $25 to over $130 by 2008.
NATO intervention in Libya removed 1.6M barrels/day from market.
ISIS captured Iraqi oil fields. Coincided with oversupply leading to price crash.
Trump withdrew from Iran nuclear deal. Sanctions on Iranian oil exports.
Houthi drone attack on Saudi oil facilities. Largest single supply disruption ever.
Saudi Arabia flooded market after Russia refused OPEC+ cuts. Oil crashed to $18.
Russian invasion of Ukraine. Western sanctions on Russian energy. Brent hit $128.
Hamas attack on Israel triggered regional conflict. Risk premium on oil.
Iran-Israel tensions and broader regional instability. Shipping disruptions in Red Sea.
